annotations.label: Lie group

anchor.snippet: a Lie group (pronounced / l iː / Lee ) is a group that is also a differentiable manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib's `LieGroup I n G` defines a group that is also a `C^n` manifold with smooth multiplication and inversion, generalizing the standard Lie group notion.

annotations.label: Real Lie group

anchor.snippet: A real Lie group is a group that is also a finite-dimensional real smooth manifold

annotations.note: `LieGroup` covers Lie groups over any `NontriviallyNormedField` and is not restricted to finite-dimensional real manifolds, so the specifically real finite-dimensional notion is only a special case.

annotations.label: General linear group GL(2,R)

anchor.snippet: The 2×2 real invertible matrices form a group under multiplication, called general linear group of degree 2

annotations.note: `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup n R` (notation `GL n R`) defines the group of invertible matrices for any `n` and ring `R`, of which GL(2,ℝ) is an instance.

annotations.label: Rotation group SO(2)

anchor.snippet: The rotation matrices form a subgroup

annotations.note: `Matrix.specialOrthogonalGroup n R` is defined, with a specialization `mem_specialOrthogonalGroup_fin_two_iff` for SO(2).

annotations.label: Affine group of one dimension

anchor.snippet: The affine group of one dimension is a two-dimensional matrix Lie group

annotations.note: No `AffineGroup` definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Irrational winding on the torus (non-Lie subgroup)

anchor.snippet: We now present an example of a group with an uncountable number of elements that is not a Lie group under a certain topology

annotations.note: No formalization of the irrational winding/dense line on the torus as a non-Lie subgroup was found.

annotations.label: Matrix Lie group

anchor.snippet: Lie groups of this sort are called matrix Lie groups

annotations.note: Mathlib has matrix groups (GL, SL, unitary, orthogonal, symplectic) but no abstract notion of `MatrixLieGroup` as a closed subgroup of GL_n(ℂ).

annotations.label: Special linear groups SL(n,R), SL(n,C)

anchor.snippet: The special linear groups over

annotations.note: `Matrix.SpecialLinearGroup n R` is the group of `n × n` matrices with determinant 1 over a ring `R`.

annotations.label: Unitary and special unitary groups

anchor.snippet: The unitary groups and special unitary groups

annotations.note: `Matrix.unitaryGroup` and `Matrix.specialUnitaryGroup` are defined as submonoids of matrices over a star ring.

annotations.label: Orthogonal and special orthogonal groups

anchor.snippet: The orthogonal groups and special orthogonal groups

annotations.note: `Matrix.orthogonalGroup n R` and `Matrix.specialOrthogonalGroup n R` are defined as abbreviations for the corresponding unitary groups under the commutative-star instance.

annotations.label: Complex Lie group

anchor.snippet: A complex Lie group is defined in the same way using complex manifolds rather than real ones

annotations.note: `LieGroup` is defined over any `NontriviallyNormedField` including ℂ, but there is no separate `ComplexLieGroup` class requiring a holomorphic atlas.

annotations.label: p-adic Lie group

anchor.snippet: one can define a p -adic Lie group over the p -adic numbers

annotations.note: `LieGroup` is generic over a nontrivially normed field so the definition applies to ℚ_p, but no specific p-adic Lie group theory is developed.

annotations.label: Gleason–Montgomery–Zippin (Hilbert's fifth problem)

anchor.snippet: in 1952, Gleason , Montgomery and Zippin showed that if G is a topological manifold with continuous group operations, then there exists exactly one analytic structure

annotations.note: Hilbert's fifth problem / Gleason–Montgomery–Zippin theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Categorical definition of Lie group

anchor.snippet: a Lie group is a group object in the category of smooth manifolds

annotations.note: No formalization of Lie groups as group objects in the category of smooth manifolds was found.

annotations.label: Topological definition of Lie group

anchor.snippet: A Lie group can be defined as a ( Hausdorff ) topological group that, near the identity element, looks like a transformation group

annotations.note: The topological characterization of Lie groups via local transformation groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of one-dimensional connected Lie groups

anchor.snippet: The only connected Lie groups with dimension one are the real line

annotations.note: The classification of one-dimensional connected Lie groups as ℝ or S¹ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Two-dimensional simply connected Lie groups

anchor.snippet: In two dimensions, if we restrict attention to simply connected groups, then they are classified by their Lie algebras

annotations.note: The classification of two-dimensional simply connected Lie groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: SU(2)

anchor.snippet: The group SU(2) is the group of

annotations.note: SU(2) is `Matrix.specialUnitaryGroup (Fin 2) ℂ`, an instance of the general definition.

annotations.label: Heisenberg group

anchor.snippet: The Heisenberg group is a connected nilpotent Lie group

annotations.note: No `HeisenbergGroup` definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorentz group

anchor.snippet: The Lorentz group is a 6-dimensional Lie group of linear isometries of the Minkowski space

annotations.note: No Lorentz group is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré group

anchor.snippet: The Poincaré group is a 10-dimensional Lie group of affine isometries of the Minkowski space

annotations.note: No Poincaré group is defined in Mathlib (the `PoincareConjecture` file is unrelated).

annotations.label: Exceptional Lie groups

anchor.snippet: The exceptional Lie groups of types G 2 , F 4 , E 6 , E 7 , E 8 have dimensions 14, 52, 78, 133, and 248

annotations.note: The exceptional Lie groups G₂, F₄, E₆, E₇, E₈ are not formalized as groups in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symplectic group

anchor.snippet: The symplectic group [MATH] consists of all

annotations.note: `Matrix.symplecticGroup` is defined as matrices preserving the canonical symplectic form.

annotations.label: Lie algebra of a Lie group

anchor.section: The Lie algebra associated with a Lie group
anchor.snippet: To every Lie group we can associate a Lie algebra whose underlying vector space is the tangent space of the Lie group at the identity element

annotations.note: `GroupLieAlgebra I G` is defined as the tangent space at the identity, equipped with a Lie bracket via left-invariant vector fields.

annotations.label: Lie group homomorphism

anchor.snippet: If G and H are Lie groups, then a Lie group homomorphism f : G → H is a smooth group homomorphism

annotations.note: No bundled `LieGroupHom` type was found; one must combine `MonoidHom` with `ContMDiff` ad hoc.

annotations.label: Continuous homomorphisms are analytic

anchor.snippet: every continuous homomorphism between real Lie groups turns out to be (real) analytic

annotations.note: The theorem that continuous group homomorphisms between real Lie groups are real analytic is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of Lie groups

anchor.snippet: Two Lie groups are called isomorphic if there exists a bijective homomorphism between them whose inverse is also a Lie group homomorphism

annotations.note: No bundled notion of Lie group isomorphism was found.

annotations.label: Lie's third theorem

anchor.section: Lie group versus Lie algebra isomorphisms
anchor.snippet: Lie's third theorem , which states that every finite-dimensional, real Lie algebra is the Lie algebra of some (linear) Lie group

annotations.note: Lie's third theorem (integration of finite-dim Lie algebras) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ado's theorem

anchor.section: Lie group versus Lie algebra isomorphisms
anchor.snippet: Ado's theorem , which says every finite-dimensional real Lie algebra is isomorphic to a matrix Lie algebra

annotations.note: Ado's theorem on faithful representations of finite-dim Lie algebras is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simply connected Lie groups determined by Lie algebra

anchor.section: Lie group versus Lie algebra isomorphisms
anchor.snippet: two simply connected Lie groups with isomorphic Lie algebras are isomorphic

annotations.note: The Lie correspondence for simply connected Lie groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simply connected Lie group

anchor.snippet: A Lie group [MATH] is said to be simply connected if every loop in

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general `SimplyConnectedSpace` typeclass but no specific `SimplyConnectedLieGroup` notion.

annotations.label: Lifting Lie algebra homomorphisms to simply connected groups

anchor.snippet: Suppose [MATH] and [MATH] are Lie groups with Lie algebras

annotations.note: The lifting of Lie algebra homomorphisms to Lie group homomorphisms from a simply connected group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Exponential map (matrix case)

anchor.snippet: The exponential map from the Lie algebra [MATH] of the general linear group [MATH] to [MATH] is defined by the matrix exponential

annotations.note: The matrix exponential is `NormedSpace.exp` applied to matrices, with dedicated lemmas like `Matrix.exp_add_of_commute`.

annotations.label: Exponential map (general Lie group)

anchor.snippet: This is called the exponential map , and it maps the Lie algebra

annotations.note: No general Lie group exponential map (via one-parameter subgroups) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Baker–Campbell–Hausdorff formula

anchor.snippet: the Baker–Campbell–Hausdorff formula : there exists a neighborhood

annotations.note: The Baker–Campbell–Hausdorff formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-surjectivity of exp for SL(2,R)

anchor.snippet: the exponential map of SL(2, R ) is not surjective

annotations.note: Non-surjectivity of the matrix exponential on SL(2,ℝ) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lie subgroup

anchor.snippet: A Lie subgroup [MATH] of a Lie group [MATH] is a Lie group that is a subset of

annotations.note: No `LieSubgroup` notion was found; only general `Subgroup` and `ClosedSubgroup` exist.

annotations.label: Cartan's closed subgroup theorem

anchor.snippet: According to Cartan's theorem , a closed subgroup of [MATH] admits a unique smooth structure

annotations.note: Cartan's closed subgroup theorem (closed subgroup of a Lie group is a Lie subgroup) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Correspondence between Lie subalgebras and connected Lie subgroups

anchor.snippet: The exponential map gives a one-to-one correspondence between the connected Lie subgroups of a connected Lie group

annotations.note: The Lie subalgebra ↔ connected Lie subgroup correspondence is not formalized.

annotations.label: Levi decomposition

anchor.snippet: the Levi decomposition , which says that every simply connected Lie group is the semidirect product of a solvable normal subgroup and a semisimple subgroup

annotations.note: Levi decomposition is not formalized for Lie groups in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of connected compact Lie groups

anchor.snippet: Connected compact Lie groups are all known: they are finite central quotients of a product of copies of the circle group

annotations.note: The classification of compact connected Lie groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simply connected solvable Lie groups embed in upper triangular matrices

anchor.snippet: Any simply connected solvable Lie group is isomorphic to a closed subgroup of the group of invertible upper triangular matrices

annotations.note: Embedding of simply connected solvable Lie groups into upper triangular matrices is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simply connected nilpotent Lie groups embed in unipotent upper triangular matrices

anchor.snippet: Any simply connected nilpotent Lie group is isomorphic to a closed subgroup of the group of invertible upper triangular matrices with 1s on the diagonal

annotations.note: Embedding of simply connected nilpotent Lie groups into unipotent upper triangular matrices is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simple Lie group

anchor.snippet: Simple Lie groups are sometimes defined to be those that are simple as abstract groups

annotations.note: Simplicity is defined for Lie algebras (`LieAlgebra.IsSimple`), but not for Lie groups.

annotations.label: Semisimple Lie group

anchor.snippet: Semisimple Lie groups are Lie groups whose Lie algebra is a product of simple Lie algebras

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.IsSemisimple` exists for Lie algebras, but semisimple Lie groups are not separately defined.

annotations.label: Every Lie group is parallelizable

anchor.snippet: Every Lie group is parallelizable , and hence an orientable manifold

annotations.note: The statement that every Lie group is parallelizable (trivial tangent bundle) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Universal cover of a connected Lie group is a Lie group

anchor.snippet: The universal cover of a connected Lie group is a Lie group

annotations.note: No formalization of the universal cover of a Lie group equipped with a compatible Lie group structure was found.

annotations.label: Quotient of a Lie group by a closed normal subgroup is a Lie group

anchor.snippet: The quotient of a Lie group by a closed normal subgroup is a Lie group

annotations.note: No formalization of the Lie group structure on the quotient by a closed normal subgroup was found.

annotations.label: Lie bracket of GL(n,C) is the matrix commutator

anchor.section: The Lie algebra associated with a Lie group
anchor.snippet: The Lie algebra of the general linear group GL( n , C ) of invertible matrices is the vector space M( n , C ) of square matrices with the Lie bracket given by [ A , B ] = AB − BA

annotations.note: Mathlib equips square matrices with the commutator Lie bracket via the `LieRing` instance on `Matrix n n R`.

annotations.label: SO(3) is not simply connected

anchor.snippet: The rotation group SO(3) , on the other hand, is not simply connected

annotations.note: The non-simply-connectedness of SO(3) (π₁(SO(3)) ≅ ℤ/2) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Identity component is an open normal subgroup with discrete quotient

anchor.snippet: The identity component of any Lie group is an open normal subgroup , and the quotient group is a discrete group

annotations.note: Mathlib has the identity component of a topological group as an open normal subgroup in general; the Lie-specific statement about discreteness of the quotient follows but is not stated as a Lie group result.

annotations.label: Diffeomorphism group acts transitively on a Lie group

anchor.snippet: The diffeomorphism group of a Lie group acts transitively on the Lie group

annotations.note: Transitivity of the diffeomorphism group action on a Lie group is not formalized.
